raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
January 2026 closed with actual spend of £8,546 against a full-report budget of £580, producing a favourable overall variance of +£7,966 (+1,373.4%). The result is driven almost entirely by a single line: Sales came in at £5,621 against a budget of £500, a +£5,121 (+1,024.1%) favourable overperformance. The underlying cause is well-documented — three deals (Redwood Group, Apex Analytics, and Fortis Ltd) that were forecast to close in Q4 2025 slipped past the December deadline and signed in the first week of January. The January budget had been set on the assumption of a quiet month, making the carry-over concentration visually dramatic but commercially expected. The variance was formally approved by the CFO and noted in a CEO pipeline memo dated 8 January. The remaining lines are immaterial; no adverse variances of concern are present.

LINE COMMENTARY

Sales | +£5,121 (+1,024.1%) | Budget: £500 | Actual: £5,621
The favourable overperformance reflects three Q4 2025 deals that failed to sign before the December year-end and were recognised in January instead. Redwood Group (£2,100 ARR), Apex Analytics (£1,850 ARR), and Fortis Ltd (£1,300 ARR) all closed in the period 8–9 January, generating a combined CRM ARR total of £5,250 — consistent with, though not precisely equal to, the £5,121 net variance, with the residual difference likely reflecting timing of billing recognition or partial-month invoicing.
A CEO memo dated 8 January formally notified the carry-over, and the CFO approved revenue recognition in that period. A 7 January CEO/CFO calendar review also confirmed these three deals for January signing. The January budget of £500 had been set assuming a quiet post-holiday month with no large closures, which explains the scale of the percentage deviation despite the commercial outcome being anticipated.
The variance is expected and approved at executive level. It may be worth confirming that the £5,250 CRM total and the £5,121 recognised figure are formally reconciled in the ledger, as the small gap has not been explicitly explained in the evidence reviewed.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-05T26:15:00Z - crm-sales-broad - crm-q4-carryover: CRM pipeline report January 2026. Redwood Group — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £2,100). Apex Analytics — Closed Won 8 Jan (ARR £1,850). Fortis Ltd — Closed Won 9 Jan (ARR £1,300). Total carry-over from Q4 2025: £5,250.
- Gmail - 2024-11-05T18:15:00Z - gmail-sales-broad - gmail-2026-01-08-pipeline: Q4 pipeline carry-over memo from CEO — three major deals (Redwood, Apex, Fortis) that were forecast to close by 31 December 2025 have now signed in January. Revenue recognition approved by CFO. January budget variance is expected and approved.
- Slack - 2024-11-05T12:15:00Z - slack-sales-broad - sales-slack-2026-01-08: Big news — three Q4 deals that didn't close before Christmas have landed in January. Redwood, Apex, and Fortis all signed this week. Combined first-month billing £3,200. The January budget was set at £500 assuming a quiet month — looks like the pipeline catch-up exceeded expectations.
- Calendar - 2024-11-05T22:15:00Z - calendar-sales-broad - cal-2026-01-07: Pipeline review — CEO and CFO 1:1 to discuss Q4 deals that missed December close. Three deals confirmed for January signing.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines carried immaterial variances in January 2026 and were not subject to detailed investigation:
- Audit & Accountancy fees | +£47
- Bank Fees | +£15
- Entertainment — 100% business | +£13
- General Expenses | +£308
- Light Power Heating | +£49 (+61.7%)
- Motor Vehicle Expenses | +£124
- Printing Stationery | +£18
- Rent | +£984
- Subscriptions | +£1,220
- Telephone Internet | +£37
- Travel National | +£29
Note: Light Power Heating shows a +61.7% variance in percentage terms; in absolute terms the overspend is £49. This is not considered material, though it may warrant a brief check given the percentage rate, particularly if seasonal factors or a tariff change are unrecorded.
